metadata.description: Complete plan to take a raw product idea from concept through launched, monitored software product. Designed for AI agent execution with human-in-the-loop via needs_clarification mechanism. Supports both greenfield and existing project onboarding via Phase -1.
metadata.clarification_mechanism: Any agent can trigger needs_clarification status. The assistant agent formulates the question, sends via Telegram, interprets the human reply, and resumes the task with clarification context. Reminders are sent every 24 hours if no response. After 72 hours, the orchestrator logs the blocker and pauses the pipeline.
metadata.artifact_convention: Each step reads input_artifacts from prior steps and produces output_artifacts. The orchestrator must pass artifact contents as context to the executing agent. Every artifact name must be globally unique across the entire plan.
metadata.template_convention: Steps with type=template are reusable sequences. They are instantiated by referencing the template_id and providing template_params. The implementation_backlog lists items that each instantiate the feature_implementation_template.

review_policy.escalation_message_template: Review cycle for step '{step_name}' has failed {max_review_cycles} times. Issues: {issues_list}. Human decision needed: fix manually, accept as-is, or provide direction.

revision_policy.description: Allows controlled feedback from later phases back to earlier phase artifacts without full phase re-entry.

allowed_revisions.mechanism: Architecture alignment check at sprint boundaries produces mini-ADRs and updates the relevant artifact sections in-place.

onboarding_policy.description: Controls behavior when the engine operates on an existing codebase rather than a greenfield project.

conditional_groups.description: If fewer than 3 direct competitors exist, run a lighter research path.

fallback_steps.name: competitor_feature_scan_lite
fallback_steps.instruction: Fewer than 3 direct competitors found. Do a lightweight feature scan of whatever direct competitors exist, plus analyze indirect competitors more deeply. For each competitor or indirect alternative: catalog visible features from their website, app store listing, and documentation. Build a combined feature matrix covering both direct and indirect competitors. Note: a thin competitive landscape is itself a signal — either blue ocean opportunity or no viable market. Flag this for the go/no-go assessment.
fallback_steps.done_when: competitor_features_raw and competitor_feature_matrix exist with whatever competitive data is available.

conditional_groups.description: Skip realtime design if no functional requirements need it.

fallback_steps.name: realtime_not_needed
fallback_steps.instruction: No realtime requirements identified. Document this decision and recommend the simplest future path to add realtime (e.g., SSE for notifications, WebSocket for collaboration). Output a brief document stating realtime is not needed for MVP with a migration path.
fallback_steps.done_when: realtime_design exists as a brief 'not needed' document with future migration path.

conditional_groups.description: Skip payment-related steps if the product is free.
Skip app store submission if no native mobile apps.
Skip file upload security testing if no upload functionality.
Skip SEO if product has no public web pages.
Skip email launch if no waitlist or email list exists.

phases.name: Existing Project Onboarding
phases.goal: Reverse-engineer an existing project into the plan's artifact format and determine execution starting point.
phases.condition: Only execute if input includes existing_codebase_path

phases.name: Idea Capture & Clarification
phases.goal: Turn a vague idea into a crisp, validated problem statement with clear constraints.

phases.name: Market & Competitive Research
phases.goal: Understand the market landscape, competitors, user needs, and regulatory environment.

phases.name: Product Strategy & Definition
phases.goal: Define what we are building, for whom, why it wins, and what the MVP scope is.

phases.name: Requirements Engineering
phases.goal: Convert product requirements into precise, testable technical requirements.

phases.name: Architecture & Technical Design
phases.goal: Design the complete technical foundation: stack, schema, APIs, infrastructure patterns.

phases.name: UX/UI Design Specification
phases.goal: Fully specify every screen, component, interaction, and responsive behavior.
phases.note: Can run in parallel with phase_4. Does not depend on tech stack decisions.

phases.name: Project Planning & Sprint Setup
phases.goal: Break all work into estimated, ordered, dependency-mapped sprints.

phases.name: Development Environment Setup
phases.goal: Set up repositories, tooling, CI/CD, infrastructure, and design system implementation.

phases.name: Core Implementation
phases.goal: Build every MVP feature through repeatable implementation cycles.

phases.name: Comprehensive Testing
phases.goal: Ensure reliability, performance, accessibility, and cross-platform correctness.

phases.name: Security Hardening
phases.goal: Audit and fix all security vulnerabilities before public exposure.

phases.name: Documentation
phases.goal: Document everything for users, developers, and operators.

phases.name: Legal & Compliance
phases.goal: Ensure legal coverage: ToS, privacy policy, GDPR, license compliance.

phases.name: Pre-Launch Preparation
phases.goal: Production infrastructure, monitoring, analytics, and final QA.

phases.name: Launch
phases.goal: Go live safely and announce to the world.

phases.name: Post-Launch Operations & Iteration
phases.goal: Monitor, stabilize, collect feedback, iterate, and grow.

steps.name: codebase_discovery
steps.instruction: Scan the repository and catalog everything. Produce codebase_inventory with sections: repository_info (git history length, branch structure, last commit date, contributor count), tech_stack_detected (languages with file counts, frameworks detected from config files, database type from connection strings or migration files, cache detected, hosting detected from CI/CD configs), project_structure (directory tree with purpose inference for each directory), dependencies (list all from package.json/requirements.txt/go.mod with versions), database_schema_current (from migration files or ORM schema: list all tables, columns, types, indexes), api_endpoints_current (from route files: list all endpoints with method, path, middleware), test_inventory (test file count, framework detected, coverage if measurable from config), ci_cd_detected (pipeline files found, stages defined, deployment targets), configuration_files (list all config files with purpose), documentation_found (README, docs folder, inline comments quality estimate).
steps.done_when: codebase_inventory exists with all sections populated from the existing codebase.

steps.name: code_quality_assessment
steps.instruction: Assess the codebase quality. Check: linting (run the project's linter if configured, or apply standard linter for the detected language — count errors and warnings), type_safety (is TypeScript strict? are there any-types? is Python typed?), test_coverage (run coverage tool if possible, identify untested critical paths), code_patterns (is there consistent architecture? service layer separation? error handling pattern?), security_surface (hardcoded secrets scan, dependency vulnerability scan, auth implementation review), technical_debt_visible (TODO/FIXME/HACK comments, disabled tests, commented-out code, dead code). Produce: quality_score (1-10 per category), critical_issues (things that must be fixed before adding features), patterns_to_preserve (good patterns the codebase uses that we should follow).
steps.done_when: code_quality_report exists with per-category scores, critical issues, and patterns to preserve.

steps.name: artifact_synthesis
steps.instruction: For each artifact in the plan, attempt to synthesize it from the existing project. Produce two outputs. synthesized_artifacts: a collection where each entry has artifact_name, content (the synthesized artifact in the plan's expected format), source (where in the codebase this was extracted from), confidence (high: directly extracted from clear source, medium: inferred from multiple signals, low: guessed based on conventions, absent: could not determine). artifact_confidence_map: a flat mapping of artifact_name to confidence level. Artifacts to synthesize: tech_stack_decision (from package.json, configs), database_schema (from migrations/ORM), api_endpoints (from route files), auth_design (from auth middleware and token handling), frontend_architecture (from folder structure and component patterns), design_tokens (from CSS variables, Tailwind config, or theme files), business_rules (from validation logic in code), error_handling_strategy (from error middleware), logging_strategy (from logger config), caching_strategy (from cache usage in code). Do NOT synthesize: product strategy artifacts (vision, personas, USP) — these require human input. Do NOT synthesize: market research artifacts — these require web research.
steps.done_when: synthesized_artifacts and artifact_confidence_map exist covering all technical artifacts extractable from code.

steps.name: project_state_mapping
steps.instruction: Map the existing project's state onto the plan's step structure. For every step in the plan, determine its status: completed (all output artifacts exist with high confidence AND quality assessment shows no critical issues in that area), partially_completed (artifacts exist but with medium/low confidence, OR quality issues exist that need addressing), needs_execution (output artifacts are absent), not_applicable (the step doesn't apply), needs_human_input (the step produces strategic artifacts that cannot be extracted from code alone). Group results by phase. For each phase: calculate completion_percentage, list blocking_gaps (artifacts that are absent and block downstream phases), list quality_gaps (artifacts that exist but need improvement). Identify the effective_starting_phase: the earliest phase that has incomplete steps which block all downstream work.
steps.done_when: project_state_map exists with per-step status, per-phase completion percentage, and effective starting phase identified.

steps.name: onboarding_strategy
steps.instruction: Based on project_state_map, create the execution plan for this project. Determine the execution_mode: greenfield_phases_only (project has code but is missing early-phase artifacts like PRD — run strategic phases first, then skip to wherever implementation left off), implementation_continuation (strategic artifacts can be synthesized, project needs more features built — enter Phase 8 template loop), hardening_and_launch (features are built but testing, security, docs, and launch prep are missing — start at Phase 9), post_launch_operations (project is live, enter Phase 15 loop), selective_improvement (project is live and mature, specific phases needed — e.g., only security audit, only documentation). Present to human: project_state_map summary, detected execution_mode, proposed step sequence (which steps to run, which to skip), estimated effort, critical gaps that need human input. Trigger needs_clarification to get human confirmation.
steps.done_when: onboarding_plan exists with execution_mode, step sequence, and human confirmation received.

steps.name: convention_extraction
steps.instruction: Extract coding conventions that all future generated code must follow. Analyze: naming_conventions (camelCase vs snake_case for variables, PascalCase for components, file naming pattern), file_organization (where do new routes go? where do new components go? where do tests go relative to source?), import_style (absolute vs relative, alias usage, import ordering), error_handling_pattern (how existing code handles errors), test_patterns (describe/it vs test, mocking approach, fixture patterns, assertion style), api_patterns (response format, pagination style, authentication attachment, validation approach), component_patterns (functional vs class, hooks usage, prop patterns, styling approach), state_management_patterns (which store library, how data fetching works), git_conventions (branch naming from git log, commit message format from history). Output a conventions document that will be injected as context to every implementer and coder agent step.
steps.done_when: project_conventions exists with all detected patterns documented for agent consumption.

steps.name: human_strategic_input
steps.instruction: For artifacts that cannot be synthesized from code (product vision, target audience, business model, competitive positioning), collect this information from the human. Format questions based on what's missing: if no product context at all, ask comprehensive questions similar to step 0.5. If some context exists (e.g., README has a product description), ask only for gaps. Present synthesized artifacts for human validation: 'We detected your tech stack as X, your database schema has Y tables, your API has Z endpoints — is this accurate? Anything we are missing?' Get human confirmation of the onboarding_plan execution mode and step sequence.
steps.done_when: strategic_context exists with human-provided product context filling gaps code couldn't provide.

steps.name: raw_idea_intake
steps.instruction: Take the raw idea input provided by the human. Document it exactly as stated, preserving all details, aspirations, mentioned features, and constraints. Do not interpret, filter, or add anything. Structure the output as a document with sections: Original Statement, Mentioned Features, Mentioned Constraints, Mentioned Audience, Mentioned Goals. If any section has no content from the raw idea, mark it as 'Not specified'.
steps.done_when: A structured raw_idea_document exists that faithfully represents the original idea with zero interpretation added.

steps.name: problem_statement_extraction
steps.instruction: From the raw_idea_document, extract and articulate the core problem being solved. Output a structured problem statement with these fields: problem_description (1-3 sentences describing the pain), affected_audience (who has this problem), current_alternatives (how they cope today, if inferable), severity (how painful this is, if inferable), frequency (how often they encounter it, if inferable). Mark any field as 'Needs validation' if it cannot be confidently inferred from the raw idea.
steps.done_when: A structured problem_statement exists with all fields populated or explicitly marked as needing validation.

steps.name: assumption_identification
steps.instruction: Identify every assumption embedded in the raw idea. For each assumption, output: assumption_id (A-001, A-002...), statement (the assumption in clear language), category (one of: market, user, technical, business, legal, competitive), risk_if_wrong (what happens if this assumption is false), validation_method (how we could validate this). Common categories to check: 'Users exist who want this', 'Users will pay for this', 'This is technically feasible', 'No legal blockers exist', 'We can differentiate from competitors', 'The market is large enough'.
steps.done_when: A numbered assumption_list exists with at least 5 assumptions identified, each with all fields completed.

steps.name: scope_ambiguity_detection
steps.instruction: Review all input artifacts. Identify every ambiguity, undefined term, or scope question that must be resolved before proceeding. Generate a numbered list of open questions organized by category: Audience (who exactly?), Scope (what's in/out?), Business Model (how does this make money?), Constraints (budget, timeline, tech, team?), Platform (web, mobile, desktop?), Differentiation (why us over competitors?), Priority (what matters most: speed, quality, features, cost?). Each question should be specific enough that a yes/no or short answer resolves it.
steps.done_when: An open_questions_list exists with all identified ambiguities as clear, answerable questions.

steps.name: human_clarification_request
steps.instruction: Review the open_questions_list and assumption_list. Prioritize the questions by impact (which answers most change the direction of the project). Format the top questions (max 10) into a clear, numbered message for the human. For each question, provide brief context on why it matters and suggest 2-3 possible answers if applicable. Trigger needs_clarification to send this to the human. When the response arrives, parse the answers and map each back to the original question ID. If answers are ambiguous or incomplete, trigger needs_clarification again for follow-up.
steps.done_when: clarification_answers document exists mapping each asked question to a clear answer from the human.

steps.name: idea_brief_compilation
steps.instruction: Compile all inputs into a clean Idea Brief document with these sections: 1) Vision (one paragraph describing the aspiration), 2) Problem Statement (from problem_statement, updated with clarifications), 3) Target Audience (specific, from clarifications), 4) Proposed Solution (high-level, what the product does), 5) Key Constraints (budget, timeline, tech, from clarifications), 6) Initial Scope Boundaries (what's in for v1, what's explicitly out), 7) Assumptions (updated list, marking which were validated by clarifications), 8) Open Risks (remaining unknowns). Write in clear, professional prose. This document is the foundation for all subsequent work.
steps.done_when: A complete idea_brief exists with all 8 sections populated, internally consistent, and reflecting human clarifications.

steps.name: idea_brief_review
steps.instruction: Review the idea_brief against the original raw_idea_document and clarification_answers. Check for: 1) Internal contradictions between sections, 2) Anything from clarification_answers not reflected, 3) Anything from raw idea lost or misrepresented, 4) Vague language that should be specific, 5) Missing information that should be present. Output a review result with: status (approved/needs_revision), issues (list of specific problems with section references), suggestions (improvements). If status is approved, issues list should be empty.
steps.done_when: idea_brief_review_result exists with clear status and actionable issues list (may be empty if approved).

steps.name: idea_brief_finalization
steps.instruction: If idea_brief_review_result status is 'approved', copy idea_brief to idea_brief_final unchanged. If status is 'needs_revision', address every issue listed in the review: fix contradictions, add missing information, clarify vague language. Do not change content that was not flagged. Output the final versioned document as idea_brief_final with a version number and date.
steps.done_when: idea_brief_final exists as a clean, reviewed, internally consistent document.

steps.name: market_existence_research
steps.instruction: Using the idea_brief_final, research whether a market exists for this problem. Search for: market size estimates (TAM, SAM, SOM if available), market growth rate, industry reports or analyst coverage, investment activity in this space (recent funding rounds), Google Trends data for related keywords, job postings in this space (indicates industry activity). Use web search tools. Check industry-specific sources. Output structured findings: market_size (with source), growth_trend (with source), investment_signals (list of recent fundings), search_interest_trend (direction), industry_reports (list with links), confidence_level (high/medium/low with justification).
steps.done_when: market_existence_data exists with sourced data points covering market size, growth, and investment activity.

steps.name: target_audience_research
steps.instruction: Research the target audience defined in idea_brief_final. Find: demographics (age, location, occupation, income level), psychographics (values, attitudes, lifestyle), online behavior (which platforms, subreddits, forums, communities they use), pain points (what they complain about related to this problem), current tools/solutions they use, willingness to pay signals (do they pay for similar tools?), technology adoption level. Search Reddit, Twitter/X, forums, Quora, Stack Overflow, Product Hunt discussions, industry surveys. Output structured findings per category with source links.
steps.done_when: audience_research_data exists with demographic, psychographic, behavioral, and pain point data with sources.

steps.name: direct_competitor_identification
steps.instruction: Identify all direct competitors: products solving the same problem for the same audience. For each competitor, collect: name, website_url, one_line_description, founding_year, funding_total (if known), team_size_estimate, platforms (web/ios/android/desktop), primary_market (geography), status (active/acquired/dead). Search: web search, Product Hunt, G2, Capterra, AlternativeTo, Crunchbase, App Store, Play Store. Find at least 5 competitors if they exist. If fewer than 3 exist, note this as a market signal (either blue ocean or no market).
steps.done_when: direct_competitors_list exists as a structured list with all fields populated per competitor.

steps.name: indirect_competitor_identification
steps.instruction: Identify indirect competitors: products that solve the same problem differently, or solve an adjacent problem for the same audience. For each, collect: name, website_url, how_they_relate, threat_level (high/medium/low). Also identify potential future competitors: large companies that could enter this space easily.
steps.done_when: indirect_competitors_list exists with categorized indirect competitors and future threat assessment.

steps.name: competitor_feature_extraction
steps.instruction: For each direct competitor in direct_competitors_list, catalog every feature visible from their: marketing website (features page, pricing page), documentation, free tier exploration, App Store/Play Store listing, Product Hunt page, demo videos. For each feature, record: feature_name, feature_description, which_competitor, tier_available (free/paid/enterprise), and category (core, secondary, integration, admin). Be exhaustive.
steps.done_when: competitor_features_raw exists with a comprehensive feature list per competitor.

steps.name: competitor_feature_matrix
steps.instruction: From competitor_features_raw, build a feature comparison matrix. Rows: every unique feature (deduplicated and normalized across competitors). Columns: each competitor. Cell values: 'full', 'partial', 'none', 'unknown'. Group rows by feature category. Add a 'feature_frequency' column. Sort within each category by frequency descending. Output as structured table/JSON.
steps.done_when: competitor_feature_matrix exists as a structured comparison table with all competitors and all features cross-referenced.

steps.name: competitor_pricing_analysis
steps.instruction: For each direct competitor, document their pricing model completely: pricing_model_type, tiers (each with name, monthly_price, annual_price, key_limits), free_tier_scope, upgrade_triggers, enterprise_offering, pricing_page_url. Then analyze: market standard pricing range, most common model, pricing gaps, free-to-paid feature gates.
steps.done_when: competitor_pricing_analysis exists with per-competitor pricing details and cross-market pricing insights.

steps.name: competitor_review_mining
steps.instruction: For each major competitor (top 5), collect user reviews and sentiment from: App Store, Play Store, G2, Capterra, TrustRadius, Product Hunt comments, Reddit mentions, Twitter/X mentions, Hacker News discussions. For each source: source_name, review_count_sampled, average_rating, positive_themes, negative_themes, notable_quotes (3-5 per competitor). Focus on recent reviews (last 12 months).
steps.done_when: competitor_reviews_raw exists with review themes and notable quotes per competitor from multiple sources.

steps.name: competitor_sentiment_analysis
steps.instruction: From competitor_reviews_raw, synthesize sentiment. Per competitor: top_5_praised_aspects, top_5_pain_points, overall_sentiment, net_promoter_signal. Cross-competitor: universal_praises (table stakes), universal_complaints (opportunities), differentiation_signals.
steps.done_when: competitor_sentiment_analysis exists with per-competitor and cross-competitor sentiment synthesis.

steps.name: competitor_screenshot_collection
steps.instruction: For each major competitor (top 5), collect screenshots or visual descriptions of key screens: landing_page, signup_flow, onboarding, main_dashboard, core_feature_screen, settings_page, pricing_page, mobile_view. Store each with metadata: competitor_name, screen_name, source_url, description.
steps.done_when: competitor_screenshots collection exists with key screens captured or described per competitor.

steps.name: competitor_ux_evaluation
steps.instruction: Review competitor_screenshots for each competitor. Evaluate: design_quality (1-10), visual_consistency, information_density, navigation_clarity, onboarding_quality, accessibility_signals, mobile_quality, unique_ux_patterns, ux_weaknesses. Output per competitor with overall_ux_rank.
steps.done_when: competitor_ux_evaluation exists with per-competitor UX assessment and comparative ranking.

steps.name: competitor_swot_analysis
steps.instruction: For each major competitor (top 3-5), produce a SWOT analysis with specific, evidence-based statements referencing data from input artifacts.
steps.done_when: competitor_swot_analyses exists with evidence-based SWOT for each major competitor.

steps.name: market_gap_analysis
steps.instruction: Cross-reference features users request but no competitor provides, features where all competitors are 'partial', SWOT weaknesses common across competitors, pricing gaps. Output a ranked list of market opportunities: opportunity_id, description, evidence, estimated_impact, difficulty_to_address, our_ability_to_exploit. Rank by impact x feasibility.
steps.done_when: market_gap_analysis exists with a ranked, evidence-based list of market opportunities.

steps.name: technology_trend_research
steps.instruction: Research technology trends relevant to the product domain. Find: new APIs or services, AI/ML capabilities, emerging platforms, open-source tools that reduce build cost. For each: name, description, relevance_to_our_product, maturity_level (experimental/emerging/mature), adoption_risk.
steps.done_when: technology_trends document exists with relevant tech trends and their applicability assessed.

steps.name: regulatory_research
steps.instruction: Identify legal, regulatory, and compliance requirements for this product. Check: data privacy (GDPR, CCPA), industry-specific regulation (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, COPPA, FINRA), platform policies, accessibility law, intellectual property concerns, open-source license obligations. For each: requirement_name, applies_if, summary_of_obligations, compliance_effort, consequence_of_non_compliance.
steps.done_when: regulatory_requirements exists with all applicable regulations identified and obligations summarized.

steps.name: research_synthesis
steps.instruction: Compress all research findings into a structured Research Summary under 500 words with sections: Market Viability, Target Audience Profile, Competitive Landscape, Top 3 Market Opportunities, Technology Enablers, Key Risks, Go/No-Go Signal.
steps.done_when: research_summary exists as a concise executive summary under 500 words.

steps.name: market_research_report
steps.instruction: Compile all research into a comprehensive Market Research Report. Structure: Executive Summary, Market Overview, Target Audience Analysis, Competitive Landscape (with feature matrix, pricing comparison, UX comparison, SWOT analyses), Market Gap Analysis, Technology Landscape, Regulatory Environment, Recommendations.
steps.done_when: market_research_report exists as a comprehensive, well-structured document incorporating all research artifacts.

steps.name: research_quality_review
steps.instruction: Review market_research_report for: 1) Claims without evidence, 2) Logical gaps, 3) Missing data, 4) Bias, 5) Internal contradictions, 6) Feature matrix and pricing completeness, 7) Gap analysis quality. Output: status (approved/needs_revision), issues list.
steps.done_when: research_review_result exists with clear quality assessment.

steps.name: go_no_go_assessment
steps.instruction: Evaluate all research to produce a Go/No-Go recommendation. Score 1-10: market_attractiveness, competitive_feasibility, technical_feasibility, differentiation_potential, regulatory_risk. Compute weighted_score. If score > 6: Go. If 4-6: Conditional — trigger needs_clarification. If < 4: No-Go.
steps.done_when: go_no_go_decision exists with scored assessment and clear recommendation.

steps.name: product_vision_statement
steps.instruction: Craft a product vision statement. Format: 'For [target audience] who [need], [product name] is a [category] that [key benefit]. Unlike [competitors], we [key differentiator].' Also write a tagline under 10 words.
steps.done_when: product_vision exists with both full vision statement and tagline.

steps.name: product_mission_statement
steps.instruction: Craft a product mission statement: what the product does today, for whom, and how. Format: 'We help [specific audience] [achieve specific outcome] by [how the product does it].' Under 30 words.
steps.done_when: product_mission exists as a clear, concrete statement under 30 words.

steps.name: value_proposition_canvas
steps.instruction: Create a Value Proposition Canvas with Customer Profile (jobs, pains, gains) and Value Map (products_and_services, pain_relievers, gain_creators). Draw explicit connections between each pain_reliever and customer_pain, each gain_creator and customer_gain. Identify top 3 pain-gain fits.
steps.done_when: value_proposition_canvas exists with both sides populated and explicit pain-gain connections identified.

steps.name: unique_selling_proposition
steps.instruction: Define 1-3 USPs that are: Valuable, Unique, Credible, Communicable. Reference specific competitor weaknesses and market gaps. If multiple positioning strategies are viable, present all with trade-offs and trigger needs_clarification.

steps.name: user_persona_creation
steps.instruction: Create 2-4 distinct user personas with: name, age, occupation, location, tech_savviness, goals, frustrations, current_tools, behavioral_patterns, quote, primary_pain. One persona should be the 'primary' persona.
steps.done_when: user_personas exists with 2-4 distinct, detailed personas with a primary persona identified.

steps.name: user_scenario_writing
steps.instruction: For each user persona, write 2-3 usage scenarios covering first-time use, regular use, and edge case/advanced use. Each with: scenario_name, persona, context, trigger, workflow (5-10 steps), outcome, emotional states before/after.
steps.done_when: user_scenarios exists with 2-3 narrative scenarios per persona covering different usage contexts.

steps.name: feature_brainstorm
steps.instruction: Generate an exhaustive list of every possible feature from: competitor matrix (table stakes), value proposition canvas (pain relievers, gain creators), user scenarios (product actions), technology trends (tech-enabled features), USP implications. No filtering. For each: feature_id (F-001...), feature_name, description, source, category. Expect 50-200 features.
steps.done_when: feature_brainstorm_list exists with an exhaustive, categorized feature list.

steps.name: feature_prioritization
steps.instruction: Categorize every feature using MoSCoW: Must-have, Should-have, Could-have, Won't-have. Justify each. Must-haves should include: core auth, minimum features to deliver primary value proposition, regulatory requirements. Aim for 15-25 Must-haves, 10-20 Should-haves.
steps.done_when: feature_prioritization exists with every feature categorized and justified.

steps.name: mvp_scope_definition
steps.instruction: Define the MVP: smallest feature set that delivers core value proposition and validates primary USP. Include all Must-haves. Selectively include Should-haves for coherent UX. Output: mvp_feature_list, excluded_features_rationale, estimated_scope_size, mvp_value_statement. If scope >30 or <8 features, trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: mvp_scope exists with a clear feature list, rationale, and scope size estimate.

steps.name: use_case_writing
steps.instruction: For every feature in mvp_scope, write a formal use case: use_case_id, feature_id, title, primary_actor, preconditions, trigger, main_flow, alternate_flows, exception_flows, postconditions, business_rules.
steps.done_when: use_cases exists with a formal use case for every MVP feature, including alternate and exception flows.

steps.name: user_story_writing
steps.instruction: Convert each use case into one or more user stories. Each: story_id, use_case_id, epic, title, story ('As a [persona], I want [action], so that [benefit]'), acceptance_criteria (Given/When/Then), priority, estimated_complexity. Every acceptance criterion must be testable.
steps.done_when: user_stories exists with testable user stories covering all MVP use cases, each with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria.

steps.name: user_journey_mapping
steps.instruction: Map end-to-end user journey for primary persona through: awareness, consideration, signup, onboarding, first_value, engagement, mastery, expansion, advocacy. For each: user_actions, touchpoints, emotions, drop_off_reasons, metrics. Identify the 'aha moment'.
steps.done_when: user_journey_map exists with all stages, the 'aha moment' identified, and drop-off risks noted.

steps.name: success_metrics_definition
steps.instruction: Define KPIs using AARRR: Acquisition, Activation, Retention, Revenue, Referral. For each: name, formula, data_source, target_value, measurement_frequency. Define north_star_metric with justification.
steps.done_when: success_metrics exists with AARRR metrics, targets, measurement methods, and a north star metric identified.

steps.name: monetization_strategy
steps.instruction: Define business model: pricing_model, tiers, free_tier_strategy, upgrade_triggers, billing_implementation, revenue_projections. If multiple viable models exist, present with trade-offs and trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: monetization_strategy exists with pricing tiers, free/paid split, and revenue projections.

steps.name: prd_compilation
steps.instruction: Compile everything into a PRD with sections: 1) Vision & Mission, 2) Audience & Personas, 3) User Scenarios, 4) Value Proposition & USP, 5) MVP Feature Scope, 6) User Stories, 7) User Journey Map, 8) Success Metrics, 9) Monetization Strategy, 10) Out of Scope, 11) Open Questions & Risks. Add version number and table of contents.
steps.done_when: prd exists as a comprehensive, structured document with all 11 sections populated.

steps.name: prd_review
steps.instruction: Review PRD for: alignment with idea_brief_final, internal consistency, completeness, feasibility, conflicts, testability, scope creep. Output: status (approved/needs_revision), issues list with severity.

steps.name: prd_finalization
steps.instruction: If approved, copy to prd_final with version bump. If needs_revision, address every issue. Output final versioned PRD.
steps.done_when: prd_final exists as a clean, reviewed, internally consistent PRD.

steps.name: functional_requirements_extraction
steps.instruction: Extract every functional requirement from prd_final. For each: req_id (FR-001...), title, description, source_story_ids, priority, category (auth, data, ui, api, integration, notification, search, admin, billing). Every user story must map to at least one FR and vice versa.
steps.done_when: functional_requirements exists as a numbered list where every requirement traces to user stories and every user story traces to requirements.

steps.name: nfr_performance
steps.instruction: Define performance NFRs: api_response_time (p50, p95, p99), page_load_time, time_to_interactive, LCP, FID, CLS, throughput, database_query_time. Adjust targets based on product type and audience.
steps.done_when: nfr_performance exists with measurable performance targets for all key metrics.

steps.name: nfr_scalability
steps.instruction: Define scalability NFRs: concurrent_users, data_volume, file_storage, api_request_volume, background_job_volume, notification_volume. Include current, 6-month, and 12-month projections with 10x burst headroom.
steps.done_when: nfr_scalability exists with current and projected scale targets for all dimensions.

steps.name: nfr_availability
steps.instruction: Define availability NFRs: uptime_target, maintenance_windows, maximum_unplanned_downtime, RTO, RPO, degraded_mode_behavior per dependency.
steps.done_when: nfr_availability exists with uptime targets, RTO, RPO, and degraded mode behaviors defined.

steps.name: security_requirements
steps.instruction: Define security requirements covering: authentication, authorization, data_protection, api_security, infrastructure, audit, compliance. Map each regulatory_requirement to specific technical controls.
steps.done_when: security_requirements exists with categorized security requirements traceable to regulatory obligations.

steps.name: data_requirements
steps.instruction: Define data requirements: for each entity: entity_name, description, data_fields, sensitivity_level, volume_estimate, retention_policy, deletion_requirements, backup_requirements. Also: data_flows, data_transformations, data_access_patterns, data_archival_strategy.
steps.done_when: data_requirements exists with all data entities, sensitivity, volume, retention, and access patterns defined.

steps.name: integration_requirements
steps.instruction: Identify all third-party integrations needed. For each: integration_id, service_name, purpose, api_type, authentication_method, rate_limits, pricing, data_exchanged, latency_expectation, failure_mode, alternatives.
steps.done_when: integration_requirements exists with every third-party service identified, evaluated, and failure modes documented.

steps.name: accessibility_requirements
steps.instruction: Define accessibility requirements: target WCAG 2.1 AA minimum. Organized by WCAG principle: perceivable, operable, understandable, robust. Each with req_id, WCAG criterion reference, implementation guidance, testing method.
steps.done_when: accessibility_requirements exists with WCAG-referenced requirements, each with implementation and testing guidance.

steps.name: platform_requirements
steps.instruction: Define platform support: primary_platform, browser_support, mobile_support, screen_sizes, os_versions, device_types. If multiple platforms equally important, trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: platform_requirements exists with specific platform, browser, and device support definitions.

steps.name: internationalization_requirements
steps.instruction: Define i18n requirements: initial_languages, future_languages, rtl_support, date/number/currency/timezone handling, text_expansion_budget, string_extraction_strategy. Architecture must support i18n from day 1 even if MVP is English-only.
steps.done_when: i18n_requirements exists defining language support strategy and architectural requirements.

steps.name: business_rules_extraction
steps.instruction: Extract every business rule: rule_id (BR-001...), title, rule_statement ('IF condition THEN consequence'), category, source, affected_features, testable_assertion.
steps.done_when: business_rules exists as a numbered list of precise, testable business rule statements.

steps.name: acceptance_criteria_audit
steps.instruction: Audit all user story acceptance criteria: has Given/When/Then, happy path covered, error/edge case covered, specific not vague, automatable, business rules reflected, no ambiguous terms. Flag any FR or BR with no corresponding acceptance criteria.
steps.done_when: acceptance_criteria_audit_result exists with pass/fail per story and suggestions for every failed story.

steps.name: acceptance_criteria_fixes
steps.instruction: For every failed story, rewrite/add acceptance criteria to address issues. Use Given/When/Then format. Ensure specific, testable, complete. Output complete updated user_stories_refined (all stories).
steps.done_when: user_stories_refined exists with all acceptance criteria issues resolved.

steps.name: requirements_traceability_matrix
steps.instruction: Create requirements traceability matrix: FR → user_story_ids → BR_ids → test_case_ids (TBD). Every FR maps to stories, every BR maps to stories, every story maps to FRs. Flag orphans.
steps.done_when: traceability_matrix exists with all requirements cross-referenced and no orphans.

steps.name: requirements_conflict_detection
steps.instruction: Scan all requirements for conflicts: FR vs FR, FR vs NFR, NFR vs NFR, BR vs BR, security vs usability, scale vs cost. For each: conflict_id, requirement_ids, nature, severity (blocking/significant/minor), suggested_resolution options.
steps.done_when: requirements_conflicts exists listing all detected conflicts with severity and resolution options.

steps.name: requirements_conflict_resolution
steps.instruction: For each conflict: minor — choose reasonable resolution. Significant — choose resolution best serving product vision, document trade-off. Blocking — trigger needs_clarification presenting options to human.
steps.done_when: conflict_resolutions exists with every conflict resolved and all blocking conflicts cleared with human input.

steps.name: requirements_specification_document
steps.instruction: Compile all requirements into a structured Requirements Specification with sections for each requirement category, plus User Stories, Traceability Matrix, and Resolved Conflicts. Add version number, ToC, and glossary.
steps.done_when: requirements_spec exists as a comprehensive versioned document incorporating all requirement categories.

steps.name: architecture_pattern_evaluation
steps.instruction: Evaluate architecture patterns: monolith, modular_monolith, microservices, serverless, event_driven, hybrid. For each: pros/cons for our requirements, scale fit, team size fit, implementation/operational complexity, migration path. Recommend top 2.

steps.name: architecture_pattern_selection
steps.instruction: Select architecture pattern. If top 2 have significantly different trade-offs, trigger needs_clarification. Output: selected_pattern, justification, constraints, migration_path.

steps.name: frontend_technology_research
steps.instruction: Research frontend framework options: React/Next.js, Vue/Nuxt, Svelte/SvelteKit, Angular, Solid. Structured comparison without recommendation.

steps.name: backend_technology_research
steps.instruction: Research backend framework options: Node.js (Express/Fastify/NestJS/Hono), Python (FastAPI/Django), Go, Rust, Ruby, Elixir. Emphasize AI-code-generation quality.

steps.name: database_technology_research
steps.instruction: Research database options: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, SQLite, PlanetScale, Supabase, Neon, CockroachDB, Redis. Match to data requirements.

steps.name: infrastructure_research
steps.instruction: Research hosting: AWS, GCP, Azure, Vercel, Railway, Render, Fly.io, Hetzner, DigitalOcean. Include cost estimates.

steps.name: technology_stack_selection
steps.instruction: Select complete stack. Trigger needs_clarification for human approval. Weight AI-code-generation quality heavily.

steps.name: system_architecture_design
steps.instruction: Design high-level system architecture: components, databases, external_services, communication_patterns, data_flow, network_topology, environments.

steps.name: database_schema_design
steps.instruction: Design complete database schema: tables, columns, types, constraints, indexes, foreign_keys, enums. Normalized to 3NF minimum.

steps.name: database_schema_review
steps.instruction: Review schema for normalization, missing indexes, N+1 risks, missing constraints, scalability, naming consistency, audit columns, soft delete, business rule enforcement at DB level.

steps.name: api_resource_modeling
steps.instruction: Define API resource model: resources, base URLs, sub-resources, relationships, filterable/sortable/searchable fields, versioning strategy, pagination strategy, response envelope format, id format.

steps.name: api_endpoint_design
steps.instruction: Design every API endpoint with: method, path, auth, roles, parameters, request/response schemas, error responses, rate limits, side effects.

steps.name: api_specification_document
steps.instruction: Convert api_endpoints into formal OpenAPI 3.1 specification with paths, schemas, security schemes, tags, and examples.

steps.name: authentication_design
steps.instruction: Design auth system: registration, oauth, login, session/JWT strategy, refresh mechanism, logout, password reset, password hashing, MFA if required.

steps.name: authorization_design
steps.instruction: Design authorization: RBAC roles/permissions, resource ownership, enforcement points, super admin, permission checking logic.

steps.name: frontend_architecture_design
steps.instruction: Design frontend architecture: folder_structure, component_hierarchy, state_management, routing, data_fetching, code_splitting, error_boundaries, environment config.

steps.name: data_flow_diagrams
steps.instruction: Create detailed data flow descriptions for every major operation tracing data from browser through all components to database and back, including error branches.

steps.name: third_party_service_selection
steps.instruction: For each integration, recommend a vendor with pricing, setup complexity, SDK quality, lock-in risk. Calculate total monthly cost. Trigger needs_clarification if cost exceeds expectations.

steps.name: caching_strategy
steps.instruction: Design caching: technology, layers, per-endpoint decisions, patterns, invalidation, session cache, rate limit storage, cache warming, failure handling.

steps.name: file_storage_design
steps.instruction: Design file upload/storage: service, upload flow (presigned URLs), organization, validation, size limits, image processing, CDN delivery, cleanup, virus scanning.

steps.name: realtime_design
steps.instruction: If realtime features needed: design technology, connection management, channel model, event types, scaling, fallback. If not needed: document decision with future migration path.

steps.name: background_job_design
steps.instruction: Design background jobs: technology, job types, queue config, retry strategy, dead letter handling, scheduled jobs, concurrency limits, monitoring.

steps.name: email_system_design
steps.instruction: Design email system: service, email types, template system, sending architecture, delivery monitoring, preferences, dev mode, domain authentication.

steps.name: search_system_design
steps.instruction: Design search system: technology choice matched to complexity level, indexed entities, sync strategy, search API design.

steps.name: error_handling_strategy
steps.instruction: Design error handling: response format, error code taxonomy, HTTP status mapping, logging rules, client error handling, validation format, unhandled errors, circuit breakers.

steps.name: logging_strategy
steps.instruction: Design structured logging: format, levels, per-request logging, correlation ID, PII scrubbing, retention, aggregation, alerting.

steps.name: monitoring_strategy
steps.instruction: Design monitoring: health checks, RED/USE metrics, application metrics, infrastructure metrics, alert rules, severity levels, dashboards, on-call process.

steps.name: scalability_plan
steps.instruction: Document per-component scaling: type, triggers, limits, database scaling, cache scaling, cost at scale. Define milestones: 100, 1K, 10K, 100K users.

steps.name: disaster_recovery_plan
steps.instruction: Design DR: backup strategy, restore procedure, RTO/RPO targets, failure scenarios with response procedures, restore testing, multi-region strategy, incident communication.

steps.name: security_architecture_review
steps.instruction: Review entire architecture for security: auth, authz, data protection, network, secrets, attack surface, dependency risk, file uploads, rate limiting, logging.

steps.name: architecture_decision_records
steps.instruction: Write ADRs for every major decision: architecture pattern, frontend/backend framework, database, hosting, auth approach, each vendor.

steps.name: technical_design_document
steps.instruction: Compile all architecture artifacts into a single Technical Design Document (TDD) with ToC and version number.

steps.name: architecture_review
steps.instruction: Final review: every FR addressable, all NFRs have design elements, no SPOFs, security findings addressed, consistency, feasibility, over-engineering check.

steps.name: information_architecture
steps.instruction: Define app's information architecture: sitemap, content grouping, navigation model, page types.

steps.name: navigation_design
steps.instruction: Design navigation: primary, secondary, user menu, notifications, search, breadcrumbs, mobile, keyboard shortcuts, deep linking, back behavior.

steps.name: user_flow_auth
steps.instruction: Diagram auth flows screen-by-screen: signup email, signup oauth, login email, login oauth, password reset, logout.

steps.name: user_flow_onboarding
steps.instruction: Design onboarding flow from account creation to first value moment. Under 2 minutes. Include abandonment handling and progressive disclosure.

steps.name: user_flow_core_features
steps.instruction: For each core feature, diagram complete user flow with all screens, transitions, success/error/loading/empty states, confirmations, undo support.

steps.name: user_flow_settings
steps.instruction: Diagram settings and account management flows: profile, password change, email change, notifications, billing, payment, account deletion, connected accounts.

steps.name: wireframe_layout_system
steps.instruction: Define page layout system: app shell, layout templates, grid system, spacing scale, responsive breakpoints, per-breakpoint changes.

steps.name: wireframe_core_screens
steps.instruction: Create wireframe specifications for every unique screen in the MVP: content zones, element hierarchy, interactive elements, conditional elements.

steps.name: wireframe_forms
steps.instruction: Define every form: fields with validation rules, grouping, multi-step config, submit/cancel behavior, auto-save, keyboard behavior.

steps.name: wireframe_empty_error_states
steps.instruction: Define all empty states (first-time, search no results, filtered no results, list empty, offline) and error states (404, 403, 500, maintenance, session expired, rate limited).

steps.name: wireframe_review
steps.instruction: Review wireframes against user stories and flows: coverage, dead ends, pattern consistency, form validation, empty/error states, navigation reachability.

steps.name: brand_identity
steps.instruction: Define brand identity: personality, tone of voice, color psychology, typography personality, visual style direction. If multiple valid directions, trigger needs_clarification.

steps.name: design_token_specification
steps.instruction: Define all design tokens as exact values: colors (primary 50-950, semantic, background, text, border), typography, spacing, borders, shadows, transitions, z-index. All in hex/px/rem.

steps.name: component_spec_primitives
steps.instruction: Specify every primitive component: Button, Input, Textarea, Select, Checkbox, Radio, Toggle, Label, Badge, Avatar, Icon, Divider, Spinner, Skeleton, Link, Tag. Each with variants, sizes, states, props, accessibility, responsive behavior.

steps.name: component_spec_composite
steps.instruction: Specify composite components: Card, Modal, Toast, Alert, Dropdown, Tooltip, Popover, Tabs, Accordion, Table, Pagination, Breadcrumb, EmptyState, Sidebar, Navbar, Footer.

steps.name: screen_specifications
steps.instruction: For every screen: component tree, data bindings, action handlers, conditional rendering, loading/error states, responsive overrides.

steps.name: interaction_specification
steps.instruction: Define all micro-interactions: transitions, feedback, loading behavior, scroll behavior, drag behavior. Include reduced-motion behavior.

steps.name: responsive_specification
steps.instruction: For each screen at every breakpoint: layout changes, hidden elements, navigation changes, component swaps, touch adaptations, font adjustments, image handling.

steps.name: accessibility_specification
steps.instruction: Per-screen accessibility: focus order, ARIA labels, live regions, keyboard shortcuts, skip links, landmarks, heading hierarchy, form accessibility, color independence.

steps.name: design_spec_review
steps.instruction: Review design specs for coverage, consistency, component reuse, accessibility completeness, responsive completeness, implementability, token compliance.

steps.name: design_spec_visual_review
steps.instruction: Evaluate design token choices and specifications for color harmony, typography hierarchy, spacing consistency, competitive positioning, visual coherence.

steps.name: design_handoff_document
steps.instruction: Compile all design artifacts into Design Handoff document: tokens, component library, screen specs, interaction guide, responsive guide, accessibility guide. Quick-reference cheat sheet at top.

steps.name: epic_definition
steps.instruction: Group user stories into epics with: epic_id, name, description, user_stories, dependencies, estimated_total_effort, priority_order. Order by dependency, value, risk.
steps.done_when: epics exists with all stories grouped, dependencies mapped, and build order established.

steps.name: work_breakdown
steps.instruction: Break every user story into implementation tasks following: db_migration, backend_model, backend_service, backend_endpoint, backend_tests, frontend_types, frontend_state, frontend_api, frontend_components, frontend_page, frontend_states, frontend_responsive, frontend_a11y, frontend_tests, e2e_test, code_review, visual_review. Each with task_id, story_id, epic_id, agent, dependencies, estimated_effort.
steps.done_when: work_breakdown exists with every story decomposed into agent-assignable tasks with dependencies and estimates.

steps.name: dependency_graph
steps.instruction: Build complete task dependency graph. Identify: critical_path, parallelizable_groups, bottleneck_tasks.
steps.done_when: dependency_graph exists with verified dependencies, critical path identified, and parallel groups marked.

steps.name: effort_estimation
steps.instruction: Refine effort estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL) with confidence levels. Flag XL tasks for decomposition. Calculate totals and identify risky estimates.
steps.done_when: effort_estimates exists with per-task estimates, totals, and risky estimates flagged.

steps.name: risk_assessment
steps.instruction: Identify risks across: technical, integration, scope, dependency, security, operational. Each with: risk_id, description, category, likelihood, impact, risk_score, early_warning_signs, mitigation_strategy, contingency_plan.
steps.done_when: risk_assessment exists with all risks scored, with mitigation and contingency plans.

steps.name: sprint_0_planning
steps.instruction: Plan Sprint 0: repository setup, CI/CD, Docker, backend/frontend scaffolding, design system, database setup, test framework, staging provisioning, developer docs.
steps.done_when: sprint_0_plan exists with ordered task list and verified fit within sprint capacity.

steps.name: feature_sprint_planning
steps.instruction: Plan all feature sprints: each delivering a demonstrable increment, balanced frontend/backend, 80% capacity for buffer, including review and testing tasks.
steps.done_when: sprint_plans exists with all sprints defined, each delivering a working increment.

steps.name: milestone_definition
steps.instruction: Define milestones: alpha, beta, release_candidate, general_availability. Each with target_sprint, entry/exit criteria, risks_to_timeline.
steps.done_when: milestones exists with 4 milestones, each with clear entry/exit criteria tied to specific sprints.

steps.name: definition_of_done
steps.instruction: Define DoD at: task (code, lint, type check, tests, self-review), story (all tasks, acceptance criteria, code review, no critical bugs, deployed to staging), sprint (all stories, regression suite, no critical bugs, stable staging), release (all sprints, security audit, perf targets, docs, legal, infra).
steps.done_when: definition_of_done exists with DoD for task, story, sprint, and release levels.

steps.name: project_plan_review
steps.instruction: Review project plan: all stories assigned, dependencies respected, balanced loads, realistic milestones, risk mitigations as tasks, testing in every sprint, buffer exists. If timeline unrealistic, trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: project_plan_review_result exists. Timeline is realistic or human has approved adjusted scope.

steps.instruction: Execute step 'cd_pipeline_staging' as defined in the v1 plan specification. Follow all original instructions for this step.
steps.done_when: cd_staging_config exists and meets the step's done_when criteria.

steps.name: cd_pipeline_production
steps.instruction: Execute step 'cd_pipeline_production' as defined in the v1 plan specification. Follow all original instructions for this step.
steps.done_when: cd_production_config exists and meets the step's done_when criteria.

steps.name: staging_provisioning
steps.instruction: Execute step 'staging_provisioning' as defined in the v1 plan specification. Follow all original instructions for this step.
steps.done_when: staging_environment exists and meets the step's done_when criteria.

steps.name: staging_deployment_test
steps.instruction: Execute step 'staging_deployment_test' as defined in the v1 plan specification. Follow all original instructions for this step.
steps.done_when: staging_deployment_verified exists and meets the step's done_when criteria.

steps.name: sprint_0_smoke_test
steps.instruction: Execute step 'sprint_0_smoke_test' as defined in the v1 plan specification. Follow all original instructions for this step.
steps.done_when: sprint_0_smoke_result exists and meets the step's done_when criteria.

steps.name: developer_workflow_docs
steps.instruction: Execute step 'developer_workflow_docs' as defined in the v1 plan specification. Follow all original instructions for this step.
steps.done_when: developer_workflow_doc exists and meets the step's done_when criteria.

steps.name: sprint_0_review
steps.instruction: Execute step 'sprint_0_review' as defined in the v1 plan specification. Follow all original instructions for this step.
steps.done_when: sprint_0_review_result exists and meets the step's done_when criteria.

steps.name: implementation_context_package
steps.instruction: Create a condensed implementation reference. For each major subsystem: the 3-5 most important design decisions, the specific patterns to follow, the key constraints. This document replaces the need for implementer agents to read the full TDD. Target: under 5000 words covering everything an implementer needs for day-to-day coding decisions.
steps.done_when: implementation_context exists as a concise (<5000 words) reference for all implementation work.

steps.name: implementation_backlog_initialization
steps.instruction: Initialize the executable implementation backlog. Transform sprint_plans into a flat, ordered list of features to implement. Each entry: feature_id, feature_name, epic_id, sprint_id, user_story_ids, depends_on_features, status (pending/in_progress/completed/blocked), template_ref ('feature_implementation_template'). Order respecting dependencies and sprint assignments.
steps.done_when: implementation_backlog exists as an ordered list of features, each referencing the implementation template and its specific context.

steps.name: sprint_boundary_check
steps.instruction: Sprint boundary checkpoint. Execute when all features assigned to current sprint are completed. Summarize what was built, run regression suite, compare velocity, identify slips, review next sprint. If velocity >30% below plan, trigger needs_clarification. Output: sprint_id_completed, features_completed, velocity_ratio, next_sprint_adjustments.
steps.done_when: Sprint is reviewed, next sprint is confirmed or adjusted.
steps.trigger: Execute after the last feature of each sprint completes

steps.name: architecture_alignment_check
steps.instruction: Compare what was actually built this sprint against the technical_design_document. Identify deviations classified as: intentional_improvement (keep code, update design doc), accidental_drift (fix code to match design), design_was_wrong (design needs to change to match reality). For 'design_was_wrong' items: write a mini-ADR documenting what changed and why, update the relevant section of technical_design_document in-place.
steps.done_when: architecture_deviation_report exists and any design doc updates have been applied.
steps.trigger: Execute at every sprint boundary alongside sprint_boundary_check

steps.name: coverage_analysis
steps.instruction: Run the project's test suite with coverage reporting enabled (e.g., `--coverage` flag or equivalent). Analyze the coverage output to identify which modules, functions, and branches are untested. Produce a coverage_report artifact listing: overall line/branch/function coverage percentages, per-module coverage breakdown, and a prioritized list of uncovered critical paths (auth, payment, data mutations). Store results at `workspace/{goal_id}/testing/coverage_report.json`.

steps.name: critical_path_test_gaps
steps.instruction: Review the coverage_report to identify gaps in critical user paths (login, signup, checkout, data CRUD, permission checks). For each gap, write unit or integration tests that exercise the happy path and primary failure mode. Use the project's existing test framework and patterns. Place new test files alongside existing tests following the project's convention. Output critical_path_tests as a manifest listing each new test file and what critical path it covers.

steps.name: negative_scenario_tests
steps.instruction: Write tests for negative scenarios: invalid inputs, unauthorized access attempts, expired tokens, malformed requests, missing required fields, and exceeding rate limits. For each API endpoint and form, create at least one negative test case. Follow the project's test patterns and assertion style. Store tests in the appropriate test directories and output a negative_tests manifest listing each scenario covered.

steps.name: boundary_value_tests
steps.instruction: Write boundary value tests for all numeric inputs, string length limits, date ranges, pagination parameters, file size limits, and collection size limits. Test at minimum: zero, one, max, max+1, negative values, and empty strings. Follow the project's test framework conventions. Output boundary_tests as a manifest of new test files and the boundaries they validate.

steps.name: concurrency_tests
steps.instruction: Write concurrency tests to verify thread safety and race conditions. Test scenarios: simultaneous writes to the same resource, concurrent user sessions, parallel API requests to stateful endpoints, and database transaction conflicts. Use the project's test framework with appropriate async/parallel test utilities. Output concurrency_tests as a manifest documenting each concurrency scenario tested and expected behavior.

steps.name: data_integrity_tests
steps.instruction: Write data integrity tests verifying: foreign key constraints are enforced, cascading deletes work correctly, unique constraints prevent duplicates, required fields cannot be null, data type validations hold at the database level, and transactions roll back completely on failure. Use the project's ORM or database client for test setup. Output integrity_tests as a manifest of each constraint validated.

steps.name: e2e_suite_review
steps.instruction: Review the complete E2E test suite by examining all existing E2E tests against the coverage_report and the functional requirements. Identify gaps: user flows without E2E coverage, critical paths only covered by unit tests, and E2E tests that are flaky or incomplete. Produce e2e_review_result listing: total E2E test count, coverage of user stories, identified gaps, and recommended additions ranked by risk.

steps.name: missing_e2e_tests
steps.instruction: Based on e2e_review_result, write the missing E2E tests identified during the review. Each test should simulate a complete user flow from entry to completion, including UI interactions, API calls, and database state verification. Use the project's E2E framework (Playwright, Cypress, or equivalent). Output additional_e2e_tests as a manifest of new E2E test files and the user flows they cover.

steps.name: performance_test_creation
steps.instruction: Create performance test scripts using k6, Artillery, or the project's preferred load testing tool. Write scripts for: API endpoint response time benchmarks, database query performance under load, concurrent user simulation (10, 50, 100, 500 users), and throughput tests for the highest-traffic endpoints. Reference nfr_performance for target metrics. Store scripts at `workspace/{goal_id}/testing/performance/` and output performance_test_scripts as a manifest.

steps.name: baseline_performance_test
steps.instruction: Execute the performance_test_scripts against the staging environment to establish baseline metrics. Run each test scenario at normal expected load levels. Capture: p50/p95/p99 response times, throughput (requests/sec), error rates, CPU/memory utilization, and database query times. Store raw results and produce baseline_performance_results with a summary table comparing each metric against the NFR targets.

steps.name: stress_test
steps.instruction: Execute stress tests by running the performance_test_scripts at 2x, 5x, and 10x the expected load. Identify the breaking point where error rates exceed 1% or response times exceed SLA thresholds. Document: maximum concurrent users before degradation, first bottleneck (CPU, memory, database connections, or network), behavior under sustained high load (10 minutes), and recovery time after load removal. Output stress_test_results with findings.

steps.name: performance_analysis
steps.instruction: Analyze baseline_performance_results and stress_test_results together. Identify: which endpoints fail NFR targets, the primary bottlenecks (slow queries, missing indexes, unoptimized algorithms, connection pool exhaustion), correlation between load increase and degradation, and memory leak indicators. Produce performance_analysis_report with a prioritized list of optimizations, estimated impact of each, and recommended implementation order.

steps.name: performance_optimization
steps.instruction: Implement the optimizations recommended in performance_analysis_report, starting with highest-impact items. Common fixes include: adding database indexes, implementing query result caching, optimizing N+1 queries, adding connection pooling, enabling compression, implementing pagination for large datasets, and lazy-loading heavy resources. Document each change and its expected improvement. Output performance_optimizations listing each fix applied.

steps.name: post_optimization_verification
steps.instruction: Re-run the same performance_test_scripts from step 9.10 to verify the optimizations worked. Compare results against baseline_performance_results to quantify improvement for each metric. Verify that no new regressions were introduced. Produce post_optimization_results with a before/after comparison table and confirmation that all NFR performance targets are now met (or document remaining gaps with justification).

steps.name: cross_browser_testing
steps.instruction: Run the full test suite and key user flows in the target browsers specified in platform_requirements (typically Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge). Verify: layout renders correctly, JavaScript functionality works, forms submit properly, animations/transitions perform well, and no console errors appear. Use browser automation tools or a cross-browser testing service. Output cross_browser_results listing pass/fail per browser with screenshots of any failures.

steps.name: mobile_device_testing
steps.instruction: Test the application on mobile devices and screen sizes specified in platform_requirements. Verify: responsive layouts render correctly at 320px, 375px, 414px, and 768px widths; touch interactions work (tap, swipe, pinch-zoom); virtual keyboard does not obscure inputs; and performance is acceptable on mid-range devices. Use device emulators or real devices. Output mobile_test_results listing pass/fail per device profile with screenshots.

steps.name: responsive_visual_check
steps.instruction: Review cross_browser_results and mobile_test_results visually. Compare screenshots across browsers and devices to identify: layout inconsistencies, font rendering differences, spacing/alignment issues, broken responsive breakpoints, overlapping elements, and truncated text. Produce responsive_visual_review with annotated screenshots highlighting every visual discrepancy and its severity (critical, major, minor).

steps.name: cross_platform_fixes
steps.instruction: Fix all issues identified in responsive_visual_review, starting with critical and major severity. Apply CSS fixes, add vendor prefixes, fix flexbox/grid fallbacks, adjust responsive breakpoints, and resolve touch target sizing issues. Verify each fix in the affected browser/device. Output cross_platform_fixes listing each issue resolved with before/after verification notes.

steps.name: automated_a11y_scan
steps.instruction: Run automated accessibility scanning using axe-core, Pa11y, or Lighthouse accessibility audit on all pages. Test against WCAG 2.1 AA compliance as the minimum standard. Capture: total violations by severity (critical, serious, moderate, minor), violation categories (images, forms, navigation, color, ARIA), and affected elements with DOM selectors. Output a11y_scan_results with the full violation report.

steps.name: keyboard_navigation_test
steps.instruction: Manually test keyboard navigation through all interactive elements. Verify: every interactive element is reachable via Tab key, focus order follows logical reading order, focus indicators are visible, modal dialogs trap focus correctly, dropdown menus are navigable with arrow keys, Escape key closes overlays, and skip-navigation links work. Output keyboard_test_results listing each page/component tested and any failures found.

steps.name: screen_reader_test
steps.instruction: Test all pages with a screen reader (NVDA, VoiceOver, or equivalent). Verify: page headings create a logical outline, images have meaningful alt text, form labels are correctly associated, error messages are announced, dynamic content updates are communicated via ARIA live regions, and interactive components announce their role and state. Output screen_reader_test_results with findings per page.

steps.name: color_contrast_check
steps.instruction: Analyze the a11y_scan_results and design_tokens to verify color contrast compliance. Check all text/background color combinations against WCAG 2.1 AA minimum ratios (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text). Also verify: focus indicator contrast, icon-only button contrast, disabled state distinguishability, and link color distinction from body text. Output contrast_check_results listing every failing combination with current ratio and required ratio.

steps.name: a11y_fixes
steps.instruction: Fix all accessibility issues found in a11y_scan_results, keyboard_test_results, screen_reader_test_results, and contrast_check_results. Priority order: critical violations first (missing form labels, keyboard traps, zero-contrast text), then serious (missing alt text, broken ARIA), then moderate. Re-run the axe-core scan after fixes to verify resolution. Output a11y_fixes_applied listing each fix with the violation it resolves.

steps.name: full_regression_suite
steps.instruction: Run the complete test suite including unit tests, integration tests, E2E tests, performance tests, and accessibility tests as a full regression after all Phase 9 fixes (performance optimizations, cross-platform fixes, accessibility fixes). All tests must pass. If any fail, investigate whether they are pre-existing flaky tests or regressions from the fixes. Output regression_results with total pass/fail counts, any failures with stack traces, and overall go/no-go assessment.

steps.name: bug_compilation
steps.instruction: Compile all bugs discovered during Phase 9 testing into a structured bug_report. For each bug: assign a unique ID, severity (P0-P3), category (functional, performance, accessibility, visual, security), reproduction steps, expected vs actual behavior, affected browsers/devices, and suggested fix approach. Sort by severity. Include a summary: total bug count by severity, estimated fix effort, and recommendation on whether to fix before launch or defer.

steps.name: owasp_audit
steps.instruction: Conduct a systematic OWASP Top 10 security audit of the application. Review the codebase and running application against each category: Injection, Broken Authentication, Sensitive Data Exposure, XXE, Broken Access Control, Security Misconfiguration, XSS, Insecure Deserialization, Vulnerable Components, Insufficient Logging. For each category, document: risk level (high/medium/low/not-applicable), evidence found, and remediation steps. Output owasp_audit_result at `workspace/{goal_id}/security/owasp_audit.json`.

steps.name: injection_testing
steps.instruction: Test all user input points for SQL injection, NoSQL injection, LDAP injection, OS command injection, and ORM injection vulnerabilities. Test: API parameters, query strings, form fields, HTTP headers (especially Host, User-Agent, Referer), and file upload filenames. Verify that parameterized queries or ORM methods are used everywhere. Output injection_test_result documenting each input point tested and its result.

steps.name: xss_testing
steps.instruction: Test all output points for reflected XSS, stored XSS, and DOM-based XSS vulnerabilities. Test: user-generated content rendering, URL parameter reflection, error message display, search results, profile fields, and markdown/rich-text rendering. Verify that output encoding is applied consistently and Content-Security-Policy headers block inline scripts. Output xss_test_result with findings per component.

steps.name: csrf_testing
steps.instruction: Test all state-changing endpoints for CSRF vulnerabilities. Verify: CSRF tokens are present on all forms and validated server-side, SameSite cookie attribute is set to Strict or Lax, custom headers (X-Requested-With) are required for AJAX requests, and token rotation occurs after authentication. Test by crafting cross-origin requests without valid tokens. Output csrf_test_result documenting each endpoint's CSRF protection status.

steps.name: auth_security_review
steps.instruction: Review the authentication implementation against auth_design. Verify: password hashing uses bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 with appropriate cost factors, session tokens are cryptographically random and sufficiently long, token expiration and refresh mechanisms work correctly, account lockout engages after failed attempts, password reset flow does not leak user existence, and multi-factor authentication (if applicable) cannot be bypassed. Output auth_security_result with findings and recommendations.

steps.name: authorization_testing
steps.instruction: Test the authorization system against authz_design. Verify: horizontal privilege escalation is prevented (user A cannot access user B's resources), vertical privilege escalation is prevented (regular user cannot access admin endpoints), IDOR vulnerabilities are mitigated (sequential IDs cannot be guessed to access others' data), API endpoints enforce authorization consistently, and role-based access control is correctly implemented at both API and data layers. Output authz_test_result with test results per role and resource.

steps.name: api_security_review
steps.instruction: Review all API endpoints from api_endpoints for security. Verify: authentication is required on all non-public endpoints, request body validation rejects unexpected fields, response bodies do not leak internal details (stack traces, internal IDs, database column names), rate limiting is applied, request size limits are enforced, and API versioning does not expose deprecated insecure endpoints. Output api_security_result listing each endpoint with its security posture.

steps.name: dependency_vulnerability_scan
steps.instruction: Run dependency vulnerability scanning using `npm audit`, `pip audit`, `cargo audit`, or the project's equivalent tool. Also run Snyk or Trivy if available. Identify all dependencies with known CVEs, categorize by severity (critical, high, medium, low), and determine if each vulnerability is exploitable in the project's usage context. Output dependency_scan_result listing each vulnerable dependency, CVE ID, severity, and whether an upgrade is available.

steps.name: dependency_vulnerability_fixes
steps.instruction: Fix all critical and high severity dependency vulnerabilities from dependency_scan_result. For each: upgrade to the patched version if available, or if a major version upgrade is needed, assess breaking changes. If no fix is available, implement a workaround or document the accepted risk with justification. Run the full test suite after each upgrade to catch regressions. Output dependency_fixes listing each vulnerability addressed and the action taken.

steps.name: security_headers_audit
steps.instruction: Audit all HTTP security headers served by the application. Check for: Content-Security-Policy (strict, no unsafe-inline), X-Content-Type-Options (nosniff), X-Frame-Options (DENY or SAMEORIGIN), Strict-Transport-Security (max-age >= 1 year, includeSubDomains), Referrer-Policy (strict-origin-when-cross-origin), Permissions-Policy (restrict camera, microphone, geolocation), and X-XSS-Protection. Test both HTML pages and API responses. Output security_headers_audit_result with current values and recommendations.

steps.name: security_headers_fix
steps.instruction: Implement all missing or misconfigured security headers identified in security_headers_audit_result. Add server middleware or configuration to set: CSP header with nonce-based script allowlisting, HSTS header with 1-year max-age, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, X-Frame-Options DENY, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, and Permissions-Policy restricting unused browser features. Verify headers are served on all responses. Output security_headers_implemented confirming each header is in place.

steps.name: encryption_verification
steps.instruction: Verify encryption is correctly implemented. Check: all data in transit uses TLS 1.2+ (no SSLv3 or TLS 1.0/1.1), sensitive data at rest is encrypted (passwords, PII, tokens), database connections use SSL, encryption keys are stored securely (not in code), key rotation procedures exist, and backup data is encrypted. Use tools like testssl.sh or ssllabs.com for TLS verification. Output encryption_verification_result documenting each encryption checkpoint.

steps.name: secrets_audit
